data.title: NXP Semiconductors, Broadcom among Deutsche Bank's chip stocks for 2025
data.description:
The semiconductor market has been a tale of two stories in 2024, as artificial intelligence-linked companies have seen strong growth, while others have had uneven performances. Looking to 2025, Deutsche Bank is expecting much of the same.

"Looking into 2025, we expect ex-memory semi revenue growth to accelerate to [roughly 16% year-over-year], albeit with the bifurcation theme persisting (AI up low teens, remainder of sector up [low-single-digit percentages])," analysts at the firm wrote in a note.

"In general, after a year of relative underperformance for the SOX (~-6% vs. S&P), and with fundamental growth likely improving both in magnitude and breadth, we are more constructive on the group than at this point a year ago, albeit with selectivity remaining paramount."

Themes to think about

Looking to next year, Deutsche Bank sees two themes emerging: companies that are "broad-based" are likely to see improved growth next year and AI-related tailwinds should persist.

Amongst the broad-based semiconductor companies, Deutsche Bank favors NXP Semiconductors (NASDAQ:NXPI) and On Semiconductor (NASDAQ:ON) on the belief that expectations for the two are "at the lower end," there have been structural improvements and valuations are still lower.

"For the broad-based names, cyclical bottoms were largely found in 2024, so the focus in 2025 will shift to the pace of recovery," the analysts wrote. "On this topic, we expect [year-over-year] improvement directionally, but remain conservative on the magnitude given macro, inventory, pricing headwinds."

More to AI than just Nvidia

And while Nvidia (NVDA) has dominated the AI theme in 2024 — and to a lesser extent AMD (AMD) — Deutsche Bank likes Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) and Marvell Technology (MRVL). The pair have exposure to the hyperscalers continuing to spend, as well as creating application specific integrated circuits for companies like Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) with their tensor processing units and Traininum2 chips, respectively.

"On the AI front, we expect GPU-based accelerators to deliver yet another year of outsized growth (led by NVDA, and somewhat AMD), with ASICs and Networking players also contributing (AVGO, NXPI, ALAB, etc.)," the analysts added.

data.title: Earnings week ahead: ORCL, GME, ADBE, COST, AVGO, M and more
data.description:
Despite a light earnings calendar next week, several high-profile companies are set to report their financial figures. The results could significantly influence market sentiment across key sectors such as enterprise software and consumer staples.

Notably, investors are eager for updates from Oracle (ORCL), GameStop (GME), Adobe (ADBE), Costco Wholesale (COST), Broadcom (AVGO) and Macy's (M), among others.

Below is a rundown of major quarterly updates anticipated in the week of December 9 to December 12:

Monday, December 9

Oracle (ORCL)

Texas-based software giant Oracle (ORCL) is set to release its quarterly results after the market closes on Monday, with analysts forecasting over 9% growth in both revenue and profits.

Ahead of the earnings report, Guggenheim and Jefferies raised their price targets for the IT giant. Jefferies analyst Brent Thill increased his target to $220 (from $190), citing recent checks that suggest a "modest pipeline improvement." Guggenheim's John DiFucci reiterated his Buy rating and raised his price target to $220 (from $200), highlighting increased traction with Oracle Database through partnerships with hyperscalers, including several deals with Google Cloud and a new agreement with Amazon Web Services, according to a partner.

While Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system maintains a cautious approach with a Hold rating, Wall Street analysts are more optimistic, with a consensus Buy recommendation.

SA author Noah’s Arc Capital Management, who holds a Strong Buy view on Oracle, states, "Despite competition from Epic Systems, Oracle's versatile, AI-driven database architecture positions it well in the healthcare market. Oracle's strategic investments in healthcare and AI, along with its robust financial performance, promise sustained growth and long-term value for shareholders."

Another SA author, Oliver Rodzianko, who recommends holding the stock, argues that Oracle is overvalued. He projects a 5% expected CAGR over the next decade and a -45% margin of safety, suggesting it may not be prudent to initiate or add positions.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $1.48
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $14.12B
  • Earnings Insight: Oracle has exceeded EPS estimates in 7 of the past 7 quarters, and revenue expectations in only 3 of those reports.

Also reporting: C3.ai (AI), MongoDB (MDB), Hello Group (MOMO), Toll Brothers (TOL), Vail Resorts (MTN), Yext (YEXT), HealthEquity (HQY) and more.

Tuesday, December 10

GameStop (GME)

Video game retailer GameStop (GME) is set to release its earnings after the closing bell on Tuesday. The stock has experienced a remarkable rally, surging nearly 92% over the past 12 months and 63% on a YTD basis, fueled by a resurgence in its meme stock status earlier this year, largely driven by influencer Keith Gill, also known as 'Roaring Kitty.'

Last Thursday afternoon, GME shares surged 10% after Gill posted a cryptic image on social media platform X (formerly Twitter). GameStop’s shares were temporarily halted for volatility but resumed trading shortly afterward.

Wall Street analysts remain generally bearish on the stock, with a consensus Strong Sell rating, while Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system has upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold ahead of its earnings results.

SA contributor Bernard Zambonin highlights that, despite declining sales, GameStop boasts a strong cash position with no debt, largely thanks to equity sales driven by retail investors during meme stock surges. The company is focusing on omnichannel retail excellence, cost management, and brand equity, with potential investments beyond gaming under CEO Ryan Cohen, the analyst stated.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: -$0.03
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $887.68M
  • Earnings Insight: GameStop has beaten EPS estimates in 4 of the past 8 quarters, and revenue expectations in only 2 of those reports.

Also reporting: Stitch Fix (SFIX), AutoZone (AZO), Dave & Buster's Entertainment (PLAY), United Natural Foods (UNFI), Avid Bioservices (CDMO), Ollie's Bargain Outlet Holdings (OLLI), Academy Sports and Outdoors (ASO), Designer Brands (DBI) and more.

Wednesday, December 11

Adobe (ADBE)

Adobe (ADBE) is slated to announce its results on Wednesday after the market closes. Analysts are expecting nearly 10% Y/Y growth in both revenue and earnings.

Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system recently downgraded Adobe from Strong Buy to Hold, just a couple of days before the earnings report, while Wall Street analysts maintain a consensus Buy.

SA contributor Daniel Schonberger argues that, despite Adobe's robust growth rates, the stock remains a "Hold" due to its current overvaluation and slowing growth projections. He highlights that Adobe's economic moat, driven by brand strength and high switching costs, supports stable revenue growth and pricing power. However, an intrinsic value calculation suggests Adobe is slightly overvalued, making it prudent to wait for a potential dip in stock price.

In contrast, fellow SA author Mike Zaccardi, CFA, maintains a Buy rating on Adobe. While acknowledging mixed valuation metrics, he points out that Adobe's PEG ratio suggests it may be undervalued. Zaccardi also highlights key risks, such as competition and potential macroeconomic downturns, but believes Adobe's ongoing earnings growth and potential bullish technical indicators present an attractive opportunity.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $4.67
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $5.54B
  • Earnings Insight: Adobe has exceeded EPS consistently in all the past 8 quarters and revenue estimates in 7 of those reports.

Also reporting: Macy's (M), Uranium Energy (UEC), Maxeon Solar Technologies (MAXN), Nordson Corporation (NDSN), Photronics (PLAB), Vera Bradley (VRA) and more.

Thursday, December 12

Costco Wholesale Corp. (COST)

Costco (COST) is expected to report its results on Thursday, with analysts anticipating a Y/Y surge in both top and bottom lines for the big-box retailer, which recently disclosed a 5.6% increase in November revenues.

While Wall Street analysts express optimism with a Buy rating, Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system has issued a more conservative Hold rating.

Notably, SA author Joseph Parrish highlights that despite Costco's strong fundamentals, its valuation appears inflated, with a P/E ratio nearing 60—driven more by market sentiment than actual financial performance. Parrish notes that Costco faces challenges in competing within the e-commerce and grocery markets, which limits its ability to significantly increase market share. As a result, he maintains a Hold rating.

  • Consensus EPS Estimates: $3.78
  • Consensus Revenue Estimates: $62.12B
  • Earnings Insight: Costco has exceeded revenue expectations 3 of the past 8 quarters, missing EPS estimates only twice in that span.

Also reporting: Broadcom (AVGO), RH (RH), Ciena Corporation (CIEN), Anavex Life Sciences (AVXL), National Beverage (FIZZ), Lee Enterprises (LEE), The Lovesac Company (LOVE) and more.

Friday, December 13

No significant earnings reports scheduled.

data.title: Syrian government falls to rebels, rule of Assad ends
data.description:
The rule of the Assad family in Syria has ended after 50 years as rebels took control of Damascus Sunday.

Syrian state television aired a video statement by a group of men saying that President Bashar Assad had been overthrown and all prisoners had been set free.

The man who read the statement said the opposition group, known as the Operations Room to Conquer Damascus, called on all rebel fighters and citizens to preserve the institutions of “the free Syrian state.”

The statement emerged hours after the head of a Syrian opposition war monitor said Assad had left the country for an undisclosed location, fleeing ahead of insurgents who said they had entered Damascus following a remarkably swift advance across the country.

Many of the capital’s residents were in disbelief at the speed at which Assad lost his hold on the country after nearly 14 years of civil war that killed hundreds of thousands of people, displaced half the country's prewar population of 23 million and drew in several foreign powers.

As daylight broke over Damascus, crowds gathered to pray in the city’s mosques and to celebrate in the squares, chanting “God is great.” People also chanted anti-Assad slogans and honked car horns. Teen boys picked up weapons that had apparently been discarded by security forces and fired them in the air.

Revelers filled Umayyad Square in the city center, where the Defense Ministry is located. Men fired celebratory gunshots into the air and some waved the three-starred Syrian flag that predates the Assad government and was adopted by the revolutionaries. A few kilometers (miles) away, Syrians stormed the presidential palace, tearing up portraits of the toppled president.

Syrian Prime Minister Mohammed Ghazi Jalali said in a video statement that the government was ready to “extend its hand” to the opposition and turn its functions over to a transitional government. A video shared on Syrian opposition media showed a group of armed men escorting him out of his office and to the Four Seasons hotel on Sunday.

The prime minister had earlier told the Al Arabiya news network that he does not know where Assad and the defense minister are. He said he lost communication with Assad late Saturday.

Rami Abdurrahman of the Syrian Observatory for Human Rights told The Associated Press that Assad took a flight Sunday from Damascus.

A senior diplomat from the United Arab Emirates, which had sought to rehabilitate Assad's image and has welcomed high-profile exiles in recent years, declined to comment on his whereabouts when asked by reporters at a conference in Bahrain.

Anwar Gargash said Assad's destination at this point is a “footnote in history,” comparing it to the long exile of German Kaiser Wilhelm II after World War I.

Assad has been accused of war crimes and crimes against humanity during the war, including a 2013 chemical weapons attack on the outskirts of the capital.

There was no immediate comment from Iran, which had been Assad’s staunchest supporter. The Iranian Embassy in Damascus was ransacked after apparently having been abandoned. AP footage showed broken windows and documents scattered in the entryway.

Opposition forces had not reached Damascus since 2018, when Syrian troops recaptured areas on the outskirts of the capital following a yearslong siege.

-- The Associated Press contributed to this report

data.title: Goldman Sachs pulls out of global climate coalition for banks
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) said this week it has quit one of the top international green energy banking alliances, a signal of declining interest on Wall Street in climate activism against a backdrop of increasing pressure from Republicans.

Goldman (GS) became the biggest bank to announce its departure from the United Nations-backed Net-Zero Banking Alliance, without explaining why it would be leaving the coalition but saying it will continue to focus on its sustainability goals.

The bank insisted it has made "significant progress" on its net-zero goals and said "our priorities remain to help our clients achieve their sustainability goals and to measure and report on our progress."

The NZBA was launched in 2021 in an attempt to bring global banks and institutions on board with setting climate targets in alignment with the Paris Agreement; banks joining the coalition agreed to align with the aim of reaching net-zero emissions by 2050, set targets to get them there and publish progress on their efforts each year, which Goldman Sachs said it will continue to do.

It is unclear what will happen to U.S. rules regarding climate-related disclosures under President-elect Trump, but many large U.S. firms including Goldman Sachs (GS) will still need to disclose under European Union rules.

ETFs: (XLE), (XOP), (AMLP), (VDE), (OIH), (IXC)

data.title: Allianz pauses talks to combine investment management business with Amundi: report
Canadian Western Bank blames legal claim for earnings delay
data.description:
• Canadian Western Bank (TSX:CWB:CA) said on Saturday it had delayed the release of its Q4 results due to the receipt of a legal claim against a subsidiary that needed "prudent investigation."
  • The decision was taken due to a legal claim against CWB Maxium Financial Ltd., a unit of Canadian Western Bank.
  • According to the bank, the claim contains "allegations of unethical conduct by a named individual."
  • "The self-represented claimant seeks damages in the amount of $18 Million, as well as general and punitive damages, and costs, related to the provision of certain loans to corporations affiliated with the claimant and the resolution of these loans, which is occurring through a court-approved receivership process," said Canadian Western Bank.
  • The banks said that it does not expect the outcome of the claim to have a material effect on the consolidated financial statements.
  • The bank added that the decision to reschedule its earnings was unrelated to its potential acquisition by National Bank of Canada.
  • Canadian Western Bank announced the delay of its Q4 results on Friday, with the earnings report now expected by the middle of December.
  • Press Release

data.title: Executive reshuffles: INTC, ZS, ETSY and SEDG
data.description:
Market watchers, throughout the week, observed several Wall Street executives stepping up for new roles as part of leadership reshuffling.

Meanwhile, some companies such as Intel, Zscaler and Etsy added new positions to steer growth prospects.

Here are some of the recent management changes that took place during the week:

Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) made the surprising announcement on Monday that CEO Pat Gelsinger is retiring and will be replaced by co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus. Gelsinger is also stepping down from the company's board of directors. Both departures are effective Dec. 1.

Zscaler (NASDAQ:ZS) CFO Remo Canessa is retiring after serving in the position for nearly eight years.

Etsy (NASDAQ:ETSY) said Lanny Baker will join the company as CFO. Baker, who will succeed Rachel Glaser, is currently COO and previously CFO of Eventbrite.

SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG) said that it appointed Chief Marketing Officer Shuki Nir as its new CEO effective immediately, succeeding Ronen Faier, who has served as interim CEO since August.

Humana (HUM) has announced that Celeste Mellet will be appointed to succeed Susan Diamond as CFO, effective Jan 11, 2025.

Retail discount stores company Dollar Tree (DLTR) said that its CFO Jeffrey Davis will step down from the role, effective upon the appointment of a successor.

Joby Aviation (JOBY) said that Matthew Field, the company’s CFO, would be resigning, effective Dec. 13, 2024, for personal reasons.

Cerence (CRNC) announced that it has appointed Antonio Rodriquez as its permanent CFO, effective Nov. 29, 2024.

Fluor (FLR) has announced that executive vice president and CFO, Joe Brennan, will retire effective July 1, 2025. The Board has appointed John Regan as the new finance chief, effective March 1, 2025.

Beam Therapeutics (BEAM) has appointed Sravan K. Emany as CFO, effective Dec. 19, 2024. He most recently served as CFO and COO at Ironwood Pharmaceuticals (IRWD).

data.title: Trump says U.S. should not get involved in conflict in Syria
data.description:
President-elect Donald Trump said on Saturday that the U.S. should not be involved in a fight in Syria, where rebel forces opposed to the government of President Bashar Al-Assad have retaken a string of cities.

"Syria is a mess, but is not our friend, & THE UNITED STATES SHOULD HAVE NOTHING TO DO WITH IT. THIS IS NOT OUR FIGHT. LET IT PLAY OUT. DO NOT GET INVOLVED!," Trump wrote in a post on Truth Social.

Trump said that Russia, an Assad ally, "seems incapable of stopping this literal march through Syria, a country they have protected for years," because of Moscow's involvement in Ukraine.

Russia being forced out of Ukraine "may actually be the best thing that can happen to them," Trump said. "There was never much of a benefit in Syria for Russia."

About 900 U.S. troops are currently based in Syria, primarily in the northeastern part of the country, as part of a mission to prevent the resurgence of the Islamic State.

Russian forces intervened militarily in Syria in 2015, tilting the conflict in favor of the Assad government. Earlier in the week, the Kremlin said that it is continuing to support Assad.

Trump announced in 2018 that he wanted to withdraw U.S. troops from Syria, but held off later on.

The U.S. State Department did not immediately respond to a Seeking Alpha request for comment.

data.title: Crypto names Coinbase, IREN rule weekly financial movers in banner week for bitcoin
data.description:
With bitcoin (BTC) breaking the $100K mark late Wednesday, crypto names stood out among the week's best-performing financial stocks. President-elect Donald Trump's nomination of Paul Atkins, known for his pro-crypto stance, pushed the token above what had been a psychological resistance level.

Crypto fared much better than equity markets, with the S&P 500 rising only 0.96% for the week. Financial stocks faltered, with the Financial Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLF) slipped 1.8% during the week.

By contrast, bitcoin (BTC-USD) climbed 3.9% since last Friday.

Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) stock jumped 16% for the week, as investors believe it will face fewer regulatory burdens in operating its crypto exchange under a second Trump administration.

IREN Ltd (NASDAQ:IREN), which announced an upsized offering of senior notes, gained 14%.

Robinhood Markets (NASDAQ:HOOD), which lists crypto tokens on its trading app, rose 11%. The stock was also upgraded by Goldman this week, and the company's CEO said it's looking into sports betting.

Canada's Bank of Montreal (NYSE:BMO), which raised its dividend and announced a 20M-share buyback, increased 8.6%, even though fiscal Q4 earnings missed the consensus estimate.

Hut 8 (NASDAQ:HUT), a bitcoin miner and high-performance computing infrastructure company, moved up 7.9% for the week.

The week's biggest decliners were led by payment tech company Sezzle (NASDAQ:SEZL), which sank 15% for the week.

With political turmoil in South Korea, KB Financial (NYSE:KB) slid 13%.

Insurtech name Lemonade (NYSE:LMND) dropped 9.2%.

StepStone Group (NASDAQ:STEP), a private equity and venture capital firm, dipped 8.6%.

TD Bank Group (NYSE:TD) stock wilted 8.1% in the week that it suspended its medium-term financial targets as it starts a strategic review and focuses on remediating weaknesses in its anti-money laundering controls.

data.title: 9 out of 12 companies deliver EPS wins this week-Earnings Scorecard
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were up on Friday after a week marked by robust earnings and strong economic data fuelled investors with optimism.

Non-farm payrolls resurged in November, making up for October's storms, indicating that the labor market remains solid. The U.S. economy added 227K jobs last month, topping the +211K consensus estimate.

On the earnings front, out of the 12 S&P 500 companies that reported earnings this week, nine of them delivered strong EPS wins. Two companies beat revenue estimates, while only one missed estimates for both EPS and revenue.

Consumer companies were at the forefront this week after a successful Thanksgiving and Black Friday, as American consumers continued to turn out in droves to shop. Spending reached a record $10.8B on Black Friday, according to Adobe's data, marking a Y/Y rise of 10.2%. That came after a total spend of $6.1B on Thanksgiving.

Seven out of eight consumer companies that reported earnings this week, beat estimates, alleviating worries over the state of the U.S. economy, which was widely cited as one of the top concerns among voters during the election.

Companies such as athleisure retailer, Lululemon delivered solid quarterly results, with better-than-expected profit and sales, and double-digit comparable sales growth in its international markets.

The company also raised its FY24 guidance above Wall Street’s expectations and raised its share buyback program by another billion dollars.

Along similar lines, another consumer giant, Dollar Tree (NASDAQ:DLTR) also beat top and bottom-line estimates for the third quarter, and updated its forecast. For the fiscal year 2024, adjusted earnings per share are now expected in the $5.31 to $5.51 range, revised from $5.20 to $5.60.Net sales are expected to be $30.7B to $30.9B.

In contrast to these upbeat forecasts, retail giant Kroger (NYSE:KR) missed its revenue estimates due to the impact of lower fuel prices and the sale of its specialty pharmacy business. Kroger (NYSE:KR) also narrowed its EPS guidance to a range of $4.35 to $4.45 per share from $4.30 to $4.50 per share.

Three companies from the technology sector also reported earnings this week, with two of them beating estimates. Hewlett Packard (NYSE:HPE) and Synopsys (NASDAQ:SNPS) beat estimates for both revenue and EPS, while Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) missed EPS expectations.

HP Enterprise (HPE) reported fiscal fourth-quarter results and guidance that topped Wall Street's expectations, aided in part by the artificial intelligence spending boom. For the period ending October 31, HP Enterprise said it earned an adjusted $0.58 per share as revenue rose 15.6% year-over-year to come in at $8.5B.

On the other hand, Salesforce (CRM) reported adjusted earnings per share of $2.41, which was less than the consensus estimate of $2.45. The company said the lower-than-expected earnings per share was due to losses on strategic investments.

For the upcoming week, six S&P500 companies are scheduled to report earnings, with Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) reporting on Monday, AutoZone (NYSE:AZO) on Tuesday, and Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) on Wednesday. Thursday will include names like Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO), and Costco (NASDAQ:COST).
